title: Measure lengths (cm, m)
description: Measure objects using a ruler. All lengths are measured in centimeters or meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the width of the bouncy ball?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b103f0fc1ea8c9a891969f03ad19a304d9ba20c5)


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] centimeters
hints:
The width starts at $0\text{ cm}$ on the ruler.  

Where does the width end?
The width ends at $2\text{ cm}$.
The bouncy ball's width is $2$ centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the length of the bookcase?**  

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7c1389dbe55529e3836d6981256dd49bdf75acbe)

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] meters
hints:
The length starts at $0\text{ m}$ on the ruler.  

Where does the length end?
The length ends at $2\text{ m}$.
The bookcase's length is $2$ meters.
First, let's locate $7\text{ centimeters}$ on the ruler.

Here is $7\text{ centimeters}$ on the ruler.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/99cb5d559fd6ed538abc223d90f65eabeb9b0448)

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ cm}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0dde48a22a3b4ae789322ce54f7996ef5ea8b3bb)

Which pencil ends at $7\text{ cm}$?
Pencil $\redE{A}$ measures $7\text{ centimeters}$.
First, let's locate $3\text{ centimeters}$ on the ruler.

Here is $3\text{ centimeters}$ on the ruler.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b351c52d29ba16721cca8f60097f326f7aa9eca)

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ cm}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9da903bddb99d949f71a77b0a3a087265aadb8a7)

Which pencil ends at $3\text{ cm}$?
Pencil $\blueE{B}$ measures $3\text{ centimeters}$.
First, let's locate $5\text{ centimeters}$ on the ruler.

Here is $5\text{ centimeters}$ on the ruler.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5ce1cb6e5f93c9b40f06d28f878ed59eac4aafac)

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ cm}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6d800b4bb5dae640b0795793220244f8cb2ea32a)

Which pencil ends at $5\text{ cm}$?
Pencil $\greenE{C}$ measures $5\text{ centimeters}$.
First, let's locate $4\text{ centimeters}$ on the ruler.

Here is $4\text{ centimeters}$ on the ruler.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/aa2c09d84d4d14bc073004761fb5163970ddf2b5)

All of the pencils begin at $0\text{ cm}$ on the ruler.  So, we can look at where our pencils end on the ruler to find their length.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1be54f17dfd9b29a732be996fbabab5b42a44807)

Which pencil ends at $4\text{ cm}$?
Pencil $\purpleD{D}$ measures $4\text{ centimeters}$.
